commit.message:
Release v3.6.0: Add Windows support for plugin hooks

New features:
- Cross-platform polyglot wrapper (run-hook.cmd) for Windows/Unix
- Pure bash JSON escaping for Windows compatibility
- Documentation for polyglot hooks setup

Add Windows support for plugin hooks (#134)

* feat: Add Windows support for session-start hook

- Create polyglot session-start.cmd that works in both CMD and bash
- Update hooks.json to use the .cmd polyglot launcher
- Replace sed/awk with pure bash for JSON escaping (Windows compatibility)

The polyglot script uses a heredoc trick:
- CMD sees the @echo off block and runs bash.exe with cygpath conversion
- Bash sees a heredoc and skips to the Unix section

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Add execute permission to session-start.cmd for Unix

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add comprehensive polyglot hooks documentation

- Add docs/windows/polyglot-hooks.md explaining the cross-platform technique
- Add reusable run-hook.cmd wrapper for parameterized hook execution
- Document how the polyglot works in CMD vs bash
- Include troubleshooting section and related GitHub issues

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Add polyglot hook test script for macOS/Linux

Run ./test-polyglot.sh from repo root to verify:
- Required files exist with execute permissions
- Simple wrapper (session-start.cmd) produces valid JSON
- Parameterized wrapper (run-hook.cmd) works
- Heredoc correctly skips CMD block on Unix

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Use direct pipe to jq in test to avoid variable escaping issues

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: Use single reusable run-hook.cmd for all hooks

- Remove session-start.cmd in favor of run-hook.cmd
- Update hooks.json to use: run-hook.cmd session-start.sh
- Simplify test script to only test run-hook.cmd

This makes it easy to add more hooks - just create the .sh file
and add a line to hooks.json pointing to run-hook.cmd with the script name.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Simplify run-hook.cmd CMD block

Pass path directly to bash instead of using cygpath in a subshell.
The complex quoting was causing issues on Windows.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: Remove test-polyglot.sh

Testing complete - polyglot hooks work on Windows and macOS.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: Update superpowers to v3.6.0 (Windows hook support)
Update superpowers-chrome to v1.5.4
Update superpowers-chrome to v1.5.3
Bump version to 1.5.4

Bump version to 1.5.3

- Image visibility fix in markdown extraction
- Single-directory auth (flat file structure)

Fix image visibility in markdown and flatten capture directory structure

- generateMarkdown() now reports images with alt text, dimensions, and
  prominent notice to check screenshot.png for visual content
- All capture files now go in single session directory with prefixes
  (001-navigate.html, 001-navigate.md, etc.) instead of subdirectories
- Single auth prompt per session instead of per-page
chore: fix lint errors in test files and thread-ownership

feat: support positional thread ID argument

Commands like peek, log, status now accept thread ID as first argument:
  codex-subagent peek <thread-id>
The --thread flag still works for scripts and backward compatibility.

refactor: improve CLI flag names and add short flags

- Rename --output-last to --save-response (old name is deprecated alias)
- Remove redundant --json-stdin and --manifest-stdin (use --json - and --manifest -)
- Rename --all-controller to --all (old name is deprecated alias)
- Add short flags: -t (--thread), -w (--wait), -f (--prompt-file)
- Rename --raw to --json (old name is deprecated alias)
- Standardize on --timeout-ms for watch command time limits (--duration-ms is deprecated alias)
- Update help text to reflect all new primary flag names

feat: add clean command to remove old archived threads

Helps manage disk space by removing archives older than N days.
Requires --yes or --dry-run for safety.

feat: add --status, --label, --role filters to list command

Allows filtering the thread list for easier management.

fix: distinguish archived vs unexpectedly missing threads in wait

Previously, wait treated any missing thread as 'completed'. Now it
checks the archive and throws an error for truly missing threads.

feat: auto-cleanup stale pending launches

Pending launches older than 1 hour are marked as failed on list.
Prevents stale launches from accumulating indefinitely.

fix: validate thread status before allowing send/resume

Prevents accidentally resuming a thread that is still running,
which could cause log corruption and race conditions.

fix: lock entire read-modify-write cycle in registry

The previous approach only locked writeAll, but concurrent upserts could
read stale data before writing. Now the entire mutation is atomic.

feat: validate detached workers survive initial spawn

Detached workers that crash immediately were invisible to the user.
Now we wait a short grace period to detect immediate spawn failures.

fix: reject threads without controller_id instead of auto-claiming

Auto-claiming orphan threads was a security risk. Now threads must have
controller_id set at creation time.

- Added comprehensive tests for thread ownership validation
- Modified assertThreadOwnership to throw error for threads without controller_id
- All existing tests pass, confirming threads are properly created with controller_id

fix: add max iteration limit to process tree walk

Prevents potential infinite loop if process tree has unexpected cycles
or psReader returns inconsistent data.

Add cross-platform polyglot hook wrapper for Windows support

Plugins with hooks now work on Windows, macOS, and Linux using a polyglot
.cmd wrapper that's valid syntax in both CMD and bash.

Changes:
- Add run-hook.cmd polyglot wrapper to full-featured-plugin example
- Add polyglot-hooks.md reference documentation explaining the technique
- Update plugin-structure.md hooks section to show polyglot pattern
- Move hook scripts from scripts/ to hooks/ directory (consistent with superpowers)
- Update SKILL.md quick reference table and resources list

The wrapper exploits that `: << 'CMDBLOCK'` is both a CMD label (no-op) and
a bash heredoc, allowing the same file to run CMD code on Windows and bash
code on Unix.

Release v3.6.1: Fix Windows arg forwarding in polyglot wrapper

- Add validation for missing script name
- Forward up to 8 additional arguments to bash on Windows
- Fixes CodeRabbit review feedback from #134
Update superpowers-developing-for-claude-code to v0.3.0
Update superpowers to v3.6.1 (Windows arg forwarding fix)

feat: Add Windows support using polyglot hook wrapper

Implements cross-platform hook execution using a polyglot .cmd wrapper
that works on both Windows (CMD) and Unix (bash/sh).

Changes:
- Add hooks/run-hook.cmd polyglot wrapper for cross-platform execution
- Move claude-judge-continuation.sh from scripts/ to hooks/
- Update hooks.json to use run-hook.cmd wrapper
- Update test to reflect new script location

The polyglot wrapper enables Windows users with Git for Windows to run
the hook by:
- On Windows: Executes via Git Bash with proper path conversion
- On Unix: Executes bash script directly

This follows the pattern documented in the Claude Code plugin
development guide for cross-platform hook support.

feat: Run claude judge in dedicated working directory

Implements JRV-28 by switching to ~/.claude/double-shot-latte before
running the claude process. Creates the directory automatically if it
doesn't exist.

This ensures the judge Claude instance runs in a clean, dedicated
workspace separate from the user's project directory.

Changes:
- Create ~/.claude/double-shot-latte if needed
- Run claude command in subshell with cd to dedicated directory
- Add test to verify working directory switch behavior

Release v0.3.0: Add cross-platform polyglot hook wrapper

Added comprehensive Windows support for plugin hooks using polyglot wrapper technique.

New features:
- run-hook.cmd polyglot wrapper that works on Windows, macOS, and Linux
- Complete polyglot-hooks.md documentation with examples
- Updated full-featured-plugin example with cross-platform hooks

Changes:
- Moved hook scripts from scripts/ to hooks/ directory
- Updated all documentation to reference polyglot pattern
- Enhanced plugin-structure.md with cross-platform guidance

Release v3.6.2: Fix Linux compatibility in polyglot hook wrapper

Fixed POSIX compatibility issue in hooks/run-hook.cmd that caused
"Bad substitution" errors on Ubuntu/Debian systems where /bin/sh is dash.

Changes:
- Replaced bash-specific ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0} with POSIX-compliant $0
- Tested with both bash and sh (POSIX mode) on macOS
- Updated version to 3.6.2 in all required files

Fixes #141
Update double-shot-latte to v1.1.5
Update superpowers-developing-for-claude-code to v0.3.1
Update superpowers to v3.6.2

Fix browser-user agent configuration based on Claude Code docs

- Use correct frontmatter fields: name, description, tools, model, permissionMode, skills
- Restrict to read-only tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Skill, use_browser MCP tool
- Remove non-standard fields: capabilities, preload_skills
- Update description to clearly state read-only nature
- Clarify agent's limitations in system prompt
- Use permissionMode: default for proper permission handling

Add XDG cache directory and browser-user agent

- Update chrome-ws-lib.js to use XDG cache directory structure
  - Cache location: ~/.cache/superpowers/browser/YYYY-MM-DD/session-{timestamp}
  - Cross-platform support (macOS, Linux, Windows)
  - Respects XDG_CACHE_HOME environment variable

- Create browser-user agent for delegating browser tasks
  - Pre-loaded with browsing skill
  - Has access to browser cache directory
  - Specialized for web automation and page analysis

- Add test-xdg-cache.cjs to verify cache directory functionality

- Export getXdgCacheHome() for external use

- Rebuild MCP bundle with updated chrome-ws-lib.js

Release v1.1.5: Fix POSIX compatibility in polyglot wrapper

Updated polyglot hook wrapper to use POSIX-compliant syntax for better
compatibility with dash (default /bin/sh on Ubuntu/Debian).

Changes:
- hooks/run-hook.cmd: Use $0 instead of ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0}
- Updated version to 1.1.5

This matches the fix made in superpowers v3.6.2.

Release v0.3.1: Fix POSIX compatibility in polyglot wrapper

Updated polyglot hook wrapper example and documentation to use
POSIX-compliant syntax for better compatibility with dash (default
/bin/sh on Ubuntu/Debian).

Changes:
- examples/full-featured-plugin/hooks/run-hook.cmd: Use $0 instead of ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0}
- skills/developing-claude-code-plugins/references/polyglot-hooks.md: Updated example and added POSIX compliance note
- Updated version to 0.3.1 in all required files

This matches the fix made in superpowers v3.6.2.

Update polyglot wrapper to use POSIX-compliant syntax

Updated run-hook.cmd example and documentation to use $0 instead of
${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0} for better compatibility with dash (default /bin/sh
on Ubuntu/Debian).

Changes:
- examples/full-featured-plugin/hooks/run-hook.cmd: Use $0 for POSIX compliance
- skills/developing-claude-code-plugins/references/polyglot-hooks.md: Updated example and added note explaining the POSIX compliance requirement

This matches the fix made in superpowers v3.6.2 that resolved issue #141.
Move TESTING-INSTRUCTIONS.md to docs/ directory

Add comprehensive testing instructions for development branch

Created detailed test suite covering all new features:
- XDG cache directory structure
- Headless mode by default
- Browser mode toggle
- Persistent Chrome profiles
- browser-user agent
- Screenshots in headless
- Form interactions
- Multi-tab workflows
- Error handling

Includes:
- 10 detailed test scenarios
- 5-minute quick smoke test
- Verification checklist
- Cleanup instructions
- Known issues documentation
- Success criteria

Add persistent Chrome profiles with 'superpowers-chrome' default

Core Features:
- Chrome profiles now persist in XDG cache directory
- Default profile: 'superpowers-chrome'
- Profile data persists across sessions (cookies, auth, localStorage, etc.)
- Enable agent-specific profile isolation

Implementation:
- Added chromeProfileName state variable (default: 'superpowers-chrome')
- Implemented getChromeProfileDir(profileName) function
- Modified startChrome() to accept optional profileName parameter
- Chrome user-data-dir now uses: ~/.cache/superpowers/browser-profiles/{name}/
- Profiles persist across Chrome restarts and mode toggles

Profile Management Functions:
- getProfileName() - Get current profile name
- setProfileName(name) - Set profile (Chrome must be stopped)
- getChromeProfileDir(name) - Get profile directory path

MCP Server Changes:
- Added SET_PROFILE action: {action: "set_profile", "payload": "name"}
- Added GET_PROFILE action: {action: "get_profile"}
- Updated browser_mode to include profile information
- Help text updated with profile management section

Profile Locations:
- macOS: ~/Library/Caches/superpowers/browser-profiles/{name}/
- Linux: ~/.cache/superpowers/browser-profiles/{name}/
- Windows: %LOCALAPPDATA%/superpowers/browser-profiles/{name}/

Documentation:
- Updated SKILL.md with Profile Management section
- Documented profile use cases and best practices
- Added to CHANGELOG.md

Tests:
- test-profiles.cjs validates all functionality
- Tests default profile, custom profiles, isolation
- Verifies profile directories persist across restarts
- All tests passing ✓

Benefits:
- Agents can use separate profiles for isolation
- Auth sessions persist across browser restarts
- Testing with different user contexts simplified
- No more temp directory churn

Add headless mode by default with toggle support

Core Features:
- Chrome now starts in headless mode by default (faster, less intrusive)
- Screenshots work perfectly in headless mode
- Three new MCP actions: show_browser, hide_browser, browser_mode

Implementation:
- Added chromeProcess, chromeHeadless, chromeUserDataDir state tracking
- Implemented killChrome(), showBrowser(), hideBrowser(), getBrowserMode()
- Modified startChrome() to support headless parameter
- Added --headless=new flag when in headless mode
- Graceful Chrome shutdown with CDP close attempt + SIGTERM fallback
- Tab preservation across mode switches (reopened via GET requests)

Caveats (documented):
- Toggling mode RESTARTS Chrome (cannot switch on running instance)
- Pages reload via GET requests (POST state is LOST)
- Client-side state cleared (JavaScript variables, etc.)
- Cookies/auth may persist (same user data directory)

MCP Server Changes:
- Added SHOW_BROWSER, HIDE_BROWSER, BROWSER_MODE to BrowserAction enum
- Updated help text with browser mode control section
- Added warnings about POST state loss

Documentation:
- Updated SKILL.md with Browser Mode Control section
- Documented critical caveats and when to use each mode
- Added to CHANGELOG.md with comprehensive feature list

Tests:
- test-headless-toggle.cjs validates all functionality
- Tests default headless, mode switching, state preservation
- Verifies getBrowserMode() accuracy
- All tests passing ✓

feat: add using-subagents-as-codex skill

TDD-tested skill documenting codex-subagent workflow patterns:
- Critical rule: can't send to running threads (wait first)
- Updated CLI reference with new flags and commands
- Common patterns for parallel research, blocking tasks, cleanup
- Troubleshooting table for common errors
Update superpowers-chrome to v1.6.0
Bump version to 1.6.0

Fix set_profile action by exempting it from ensureChromeRunning()

The set_profile action requires Chrome to be stopped, but ensureChromeRunning()
was being called before all actions, causing Chrome to auto-start and the
profile check to fail.

Solution: Skip ensureChromeRunning() for actions that don't need Chrome:
- SET_PROFILE (must have Chrome stopped)
- GET_PROFILE (just returns config)
- BROWSER_MODE (just returns state)
- HELP (just returns help text)

Fixes Test 4 from testing instructions.

feat: show event count in heartbeat during -w/--wait

Streams Codex's JSON output and counts events, reporting the count
in each heartbeat line:

  [2:33:45 PM] Still running... (1m 30s, 42 events)

This gives a better sense of progress than just elapsed time.

feat: add 30-second heartbeat during -w/--wait

When using -w, the tool now prints a status line every 30 seconds:
  [14:23:45] Still running... (1m 30s elapsed)

This prevents confusion when Codex takes several minutes to respond.
Also updated skill docs to warn about blocking behavior.

feat: add progress feedback when waiting for Codex

When using -w/--wait, the command now outputs a message before blocking:
- start: "Running Codex (label)... (this may take minutes)"
- send: "Sending to thread <id>... (this may take minutes)"

This prevents confusion when commands appear to hang with no output.

fix: capture stderr during spawn validation for better error messages

When a detached worker fails, we now capture stderr during the 500ms
validation window and include it in the error message. This shows the
actual Codex error (e.g., "profile not found") instead of just
"Worker exited immediately with code 1".

Changes:
- spawn-validation.ts: capture stderr from piped streams
- start.ts: spawn with ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'] instead of 'ignore'
- SKILL.md: remove obsolete -w troubleshooting entry

fix: improve skill docs and detached failure UX

- Fix policy names: workspace-read → read-only (match Codex built-ins)
- Add full path guidance for Codex (not in PATH by default)
- Add troubleshooting entries for common errors
- Suggest -w flag when detached worker fails

Use compiled JS for all CLI commands, remove tsx from runtime

- Route all CLI commands through dist/*.js instead of npx tsx src/*.ts
- Fixes background sync silently failing (#25 root cause)
- Faster startup, lighter runtime dependencies
- tsx is now dev-only

This properly fixes the issue @stromseth identified in PR #25 by
addressing the root cause rather than just the sync command.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Refactor: Extract summarizer exclusion marker to shared constant

Building on PR #15 by @one1zero1one, extract the exclusion marker
string to src/constants.ts to eliminate duplication between
sync.ts and summarizer.ts.

The marker prevents summarizer agent conversations from polluting
the search index.

Release v1.6.1: Auto-detect headless mode in containers

- MCP server now auto-detects display availability
- Falls back to headless mode when no display detected
- Added --headed flag to complement existing --headless
- Improved startup logging to show mode selection reason

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-detect headless mode based on display availability

Previously, Chrome defaulted to headed mode unless --headless was passed.
In environments without a display (containers, SSH, CI), this caused Chrome
to crash immediately leaving zombie processes.

Now the MCP server auto-detects whether a display is available:
- Linux: Check DISPLAY or WAYLAND_DISPLAY env vars
- macOS: Check TERM_PROGRAM or DISPLAY
- Windows: Assume display available (headless servers rare)

Command line flags still work for explicit control:
- --headless: Force headless mode
- --headed: Force headed mode (will fail if no display)

The startup log now shows the mode and why it was chosen.

Update v3.7.0 release notes with all changes since v3.6.2

Add skill triggering test framework

Creates tests/skill-triggering/ to validate skills trigger correctly from
naive prompts (without explicitly naming the skill). Tests 6 skills:
- systematic-debugging
- test-driven-development
- writing-plans
- dispatching-parallel-agents
- executing-plans
- requesting-code-review

All 6 tests pass - skills trigger correctly based on their descriptions.

Fix skill descriptions: remove workflow summaries that override flowcharts

Testing revealed that descriptions summarizing workflow cause Claude to
follow the description instead of reading the skill body. Changed all
descriptions to "when to use" triggers only:

- dispatching-parallel-agents: 2+ independent tasks without shared state
- executing-plans: have a written plan to execute with review checkpoints
- requesting-code-review: completing tasks, features, or before merging
- systematic-debugging: encountering bugs before proposing fixes
- test-driven-development: implementing features before writing code
- writing-plans: have spec/requirements for multi-step task before coding
- writing-skills: updated with "description trap" documentation

The description trap: workflow summaries in descriptions create shortcuts
Claude takes, skipping the skill body entirely.

Document skill description trap: descriptions override flowcharts

Testing revealed that skill descriptions summarizing workflow cause
Claude to follow the description instead of reading the skill body.

- A description saying "code review between tasks" caused ONE review
- The flowchart clearly showed TWO reviews (spec compliance + quality)
- Minimal description ("Use when...") correctly deferred to flowchart

Updated writing-skills with:
- "Description = When to Use, NOT What the Skill Does" section
- Cautionary tale about this actual failure
- Examples of good (triggers only) vs bad (workflow summary) descriptions

Updated subagent-driven-development:
- Removed workflow summary from description
- Now just: "Use when executing implementation plans..."

Updated test runner:
- Added --dangerously-skip-permissions for automated testing
Add .claude settings to scaffolds for auto-approved permissions
Fix skill description to match flowchart: two-stage review (spec then quality)

Two test projects for validating the skill with full end-to-end runs:

Each test has design.md, plan.md, and scaffold.sh. Run with:

and verifies the new workflow improvements actually work.

New test: test-subagent-driven-development-integration.sh
- Creates real Node.js test project
- Generates implementation plan (2 tasks)
- Executes using subagent-driven-development skill
- Verifies 8 key behaviors:
  1. Plan read once at beginning (not per task)
  2. Full task text provided to subagents (not file reading)
  3. Subagents perform self-review
  4. Spec compliance review before code quality
  5. Spec reviewer reads code independently
  6. Working implementation produced
  7. Tests pass

Add Claude Code skills test framework

Created automated test suite for testing superpowers skills using
Claude Code CLI in headless mode.

New files:
- tests/claude-code/run-skill-tests.sh - Main test runner
- tests/claude-code/test-helpers.sh - Helper functions for testing
- tests/claude-code/test-subagent-driven-development.sh - First test
- tests/claude-code/README.md - Documentation

Test framework features:
- Run Claude Code with prompts and capture output
- Assertion helpers (contains, not_contains, count, order)
- Test project creation helpers
- Timeout support (default 5 minutes)
- Verbose mode for debugging
- Specific test selection

First test verifies subagent-driven-development skill:
- Skill loading
- Workflow ordering (spec compliance before code quality)
- Self-review requirements
- Plan reading efficiency (read once)
- Spec compliance reviewer skepticism
- Review loops
- Task context provision

Run with: cd tests/claude-code && ./run-skill-tests.sh

Emphasize spec compliance review must complete before code quality

Made sequencing explicit:
- Spec compliance review loop must fully complete (✅) before code quality
- Added "Do NOT proceed to code quality review until spec compliance is ✅"
- Code Quality Review section starts with "Only run after spec compliance review is complete"
- Red Flags: Added "Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅ (wrong order)"

This ensures we don't waste time reviewing code quality of the wrong
implementation. Verify they built the right thing first, then verify
they built it well.

Make spec compliance reviewer skeptical and verification-focused

The spec compliance reviewer now:
- Does NOT trust implementer's report
- Is warned implementer finished suspiciously quickly
- MUST verify everything by reading actual code
- Compares implementation to requirements line by line
- Reports issues with file:line references

Key additions:
- "Do Not Trust the Report" section
- Explicit DO NOT / DO lists
- "Verify by reading code, not by trusting report"
- Changed "What Was Implemented" to "What Implementer Claims They Built"

This prevents rubber-stamping and ensures independent verification
of spec compliance against actual codebase.

Improve subagent-driven-development workflow

Key improvements based on feedback:

1. Read plan once, not per task
   - Extract all tasks in Step 1
   - Reference extracted tasks in Step 2
   - Eliminates redundant file reading

2. Enable questions during work
   - Not just before, but also while working
   - "It's always OK to ask questions"
   - Don't guess or make assumptions

3. Add self-review before reporting
   - Completeness: implemented everything?
   - Quality: best work, clear names?
   - Discipline: avoided overbuilding?
   - Testing: comprehensive, real behavior?
   - Catches issues before handoff

4. Add spec compliance review
   - Separate reviewer checks: built the right thing?
   - Flags missing requirements
   - Flags extra/unneeded work
   - Flags misunderstandings
   - Runs BEFORE code quality review

5. Make reviews loops, not one-shot
   - Reviewer finds issues
   - Implementer fixes
   - Reviewer reviews again
   - Repeat until approved
   - Applies to both spec and code quality

Two-stage review process:
- Stage 1: Spec compliance (right thing?)
- Stage 2: Code quality (built well?)

This enables subagents to do their best work with clear requirements,
opportunities to clarify, self-critique, and thorough review loops.

Update subagent-driven-development: controller provides full task text

Changed workflow so controller provides complete task context directly
rather than making subagent read plan file.

Key changes:
- Controller reads plan and extracts full task text
- Controller provides scene-setting context (dependencies, architecture)
- Subagent receives complete information in prompt (no file reading)
- Subagent can ask clarifying questions before beginning work
- Controller handles questions/concerns before subagent proceeds

Benefits:
- No file reading overhead for subagent
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)
- Subagent has complete information to do best work

This enables subagents to start with clarity rather than ambiguity.

Add skills improvement plan from user feedback

Analyzed feedback from two Claude instances using superpowers in real
development scenarios. Identified 8 core problems and proposed improvements
organized by impact and risk.

Key problems:
- Configuration change verification gap (verify success not intent)
- Background process accumulation across subagents
- Context bloat in subagent prompts
- Missing self-reflection before handoff
- Mock-interface drift
- Code reviewer file access issues
- Skills not being read/enforced
- Fix workflow latency

Proposed improvements organized in 3 phases:
- Phase 1: High-impact, low-risk (do first)
- Phase 2: Moderate changes (test carefully)
- Phase 3: Optimization (validate first)

See plan for detailed analysis and open questions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Add timing delays for \t and \n in type command

The type command with embedded \t (Tab) and \n (Enter) was racing -
the browser didn't have time to process text insertion before the
key press. Added 50ms settle delays between:
- Text insertion and Tab key
- Text insertion and Enter key
- After each special key press

Also re-checks textarea status before Enter since Tab may have
changed focus to a different field type.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: React-compatible automation with DOM diff capture

Major improvements for reliable browser automation:

## React Compatibility
- click() now uses CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent (works with React/Vue/Angular)
- fill() now uses CDP Input.insertText (triggers onChange handlers)
- Smart Tab/Enter handling: \t sends Tab key, \n sends Enter (not literals)

## New keyboard_press Action
- Press special keys: Tab, Enter, Space, Escape, Arrow*, F1-F12
- Supports modifiers: {shift: true}, {ctrl: true}, {alt: true}, {meta: true}
- Example: {"action": "keyboard_press", "payload": "Tab", "modifiers": {"shift": true}}

## Connection Pooling
- Persistent WebSocket connections maintain focus between commands
- Fixes focus lost between eval calls

## Auto-Capture with DOM Diff
- Every DOM action captures before/after state
- Generates diff showing what changed in the DOM
- Files: before.html, after.html, diff.txt, before.png, after.png, page.md
- Session captures stored in XDG cache directory

## Other Improvements
- type selector now optional (types into current focus)
- Better eval return handling for complex objects
- Multi-element selector warnings
- index parameter for select action

snap

Fix Terminal.app tab switching with direct AX API

AppleScript via NSAppleScript wasn't reliably clicking tabs in Terminal,
even though the same script worked from osascript. Use direct Accessibility
API calls (AXUIElementPerformAction with kAXPressAction) instead, which
properly clicks the radio buttons in Terminal's tab group.

Add browser tab support and improve screenshot caching

- Safari/Chrome tabs now detected via native AppleScript
- Tab switching uses native AppleScript for browsers, System Events for native apps
- Full resolution screenshots cached for background tabs
- OCR results cached by pid+title for search in background tabs
- Stable synthetic IDs for background tabs (based on content hash)
- Remove Arc support (too slow)

Improve tab switching and window activation

- Add AppleScript-based tab switching for Ghostty, Terminal, Safari, Chrome
- Use System Events to click tabs for apps without native AppleScript support
- Fix window activation to properly update menu bar (AXRaise + activate pattern)
- Change sidebar animation from slide to fade in/out
- Fix duplicate windowID crash by always using synthetic IDs for non-active tabs
- Remove makeWindowKey synthetic events that were causing keystroke injection

Auto-raise windows while cycling and start on window #2

- First Cmd+Tab selects window #2 (not #1) like standard behavior
- Windows auto-raise as you cycle through them using SLSOrderWindow
- Add raiseWindow() to WindowManager for preview without activation

Add aggressive focus handling with content area search and synthetic click fallback

- Search AX hierarchy for text areas, web areas, and content groups
- Log which content area type is found for debugging
- Add synthetic click at 300ms as final fallback for terminal apps

Fix tab selection and focus handling

- Get selected tab index from AX value attribute for accurate detection
- Fall back to title matching, then default to first tab
- Click in window content area after tab switch to move focus from tab bar
- Add 150ms delay before content click to let tab switch complete

Improve tab clustering and keyboard focus handling

- Use parentWindowID for tab clustering instead of frame matching
- Fix Terminal.app showing both frame and tabs separately
- Activate first visible window on Return if nothing selected
- Strengthen keyboard focus with multiple retry attempts at 50/100/200ms

Add app icon badge and fix hidden tab focus

- Overlay app icon badge on window thumbnails (bottom-right corner)
- Fix keyboard focus not transferring to hidden tabs (e.g., Ghostty)
  After tab selection, delay 100ms then re-focus the window via AX
  and re-activate the app to ensure keyboard input works
Add release documentation

Add GitHub Actions build and release workflow

- Automated build, signing, notarization, and release
- Creates signed DMG for distribution
- Generates Sparkle appcast.xml for auto-updates
- Supports manual dispatch with version input
- Triggers automatically on version tags (v*)

Required GitHub secrets:
- DEVELOPER_ID_APPLICATION: Base64 Developer ID cert
- DEVELOPER_ID_APPLICATION_PASSWORD: Cert password
- DEVELOPER_ID_INSTALLER: Base64 Installer cert
- DEVELOPER_ID_INSTALLER_PASSWORD: Cert password
- DEVELOPER_ID_NAME: "Developer ID Application: Name (TEAM_ID)"
- APPLE_ID: Apple ID email for notarization
- APPLE_ID_PASSWORD: App-specific password
- APPLE_TEAM_ID: Apple Developer Team ID
- SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY: EdDSA private key for updates
- SPARKLE_PUBLIC_KEY: EdDSA public key (in Info.plist)

Also need: Resources/Winby.icns (app icon)

Add Cmd+Tab interception and improved cycling behavior

- Use Carbon RegisterEventHotKey to capture Cmd+Tab (bypasses Dock handler)
- Use CGEventTap to detect modifier release for cycling
- First shortcut press shows sidebar, keeps window open on release
- Second+ press enters cycling mode, activates on modifier release
- Shift+shortcut cycles backwards
- Disable system hotkeys while sidebar is active (CGSSetGlobalHotKeyOperatingMode)
- Add launch at login option using SMAppService
- Remove maximize feature
- Fix thread safety issues with MainActor.assumeIsolated

Add Sparkle integration for auto-updates

- Import Sparkle framework
- Add SPUStandardUpdaterController to AppDelegate
- Add "Check for Updates" menu item

Note: Full functionality requires creating an app bundle with
Info.plist containing SUFeedURL pointing to an appcast.

Initial commit: Winby window manager

A fast window manager for macOS with fuzzy search across window titles
and content (via OCR). Features include:
- Sidebar-style floating HUD panel
- Global hotkey activation (configurable)
- Window content indexing via Accessibility API and OCR
- Fuzzy search with keyboard navigation
- KeyboardShortcuts library for hotkey recording
- Sparkle integration for auto-updates

Document space switching implementation

Add detailed doc comments explaining the brute-force AXUIElement
discovery technique and the focus sequence required for switching
to windows on other spaces.

Fix space switching for windows on other spaces

Use alt-tab's brute-force AXUIElement discovery technique to find
window elements on other spaces that the normal AX API can't see.

The key insight from alt-tab's issue #1324 fix: for windows on other
spaces, you must use _AXUIElementCreateWithRemoteToken to construct
the AXUIElement by iterating through element IDs. Without the
AXUIElement, calling _SLPSSetFrontProcessWithOptions activates the
window but doesn't trigger the space switch animation.

The working focus sequence is:
1. _SLPSSetFrontProcessWithOptions with userGenerated mode (0x200)
2. makeKeyWindow via SLPSPostEventRecordTo binary protocol
3. AXUIElementPerformAction(element, kAXRaiseAction) <- triggers switch

Also includes:
- Restructure Package.swift for separate Winby and SpaceSwitchTest targets
- Add SpaceSwitchTest CLI tool for debugging space-switching APIs
- Disable sandbox in entitlements (required for private APIs)
- Fix .gitignore to not ignore Sources/Winby directory

Add CGSHWCaptureWindowList for capturing minimized/other-space windows

Use private API to capture window screenshots that ScreenCaptureKit
cannot access (minimized windows, windows on other Spaces).

- Add CGSMainConnectionID and CGSWindowCaptureOptions declarations
- Implement captureWindowViaPrivateAPI helper function
- Integrate as fallback in thumbnail, OCR, and preview capture paths
- Key fix: Use CGSMainConnectionID (UInt32) not SLSMainConnectionID (Int32)

Based on AltTab's implementation of the same private API.

Show windows from other spaces in separate section

- Add isOnCurrentSpace property to WindowInfo
- Query CGWindowList twice: once with .optionOnScreenOnly for current
  space detection and z-order, once without for all windows
- Use CG window list to add windows from other spaces that AX can't see
- Display "Other Spaces" section below current space windows with divider
- Slightly dim other-space windows for visual distinction
- Keyboard navigation works seamlessly across both sections

Save previous focus in hotkey handlers before showing sidebar

Move focus saving to savePreviousFocus() called from both Carbon and
KeyboardShortcuts handlers before showSidebar(), ensuring we capture
the actual frontmost app before Winby activates.

Fix focus restoration by finding non-Winby active app

Previously saved NSWorkspace.frontmostApplication which returned Winby itself.
Now searches runningApplications for active non-Winby app to properly save
the previous focus target.

Fix crash in event tap handler

Access settingsWindow outside of MainActor.assumeIsolated to avoid
bad pointer dereference when checking isKeyWindow.

Improve focus restoration with debug logging

- Check frontmost app isn't Winby before saving it
- Use AX API to raise previous app's front window
- Add debug logging to track focus save/restore
- Don't reclaim focus if settings window has it
- Remove deprecated activateIgnoringOtherApps option

Fix focus management

- Save previously focused app on show, restore on dismiss without selection
- Remove .nonactivatingPanel from sidebar to allow proper focus
- Add focus reclaim observer to prevent focus loss when clicking elsewhere
- Set didSelectWindow flag in all window selection paths

Fix multiple UX issues

- Thumbnail caching: Always capture fresh screenshots for visible windows
- Remove dismiss button from sidebar - keyboard only navigation
- Make preview window resizable, repositionable, and persist geometry
- Add local event monitor to catch hotkey when app has focus
- Repeated hotkey press now closes Winby (toggle behavior)
- Space in search no longer triggers filtering (trimmed)
- Cmd+Enter fullscreens the selected window
